mission.title: knative: fix using KNative with ArgoCD (don't set `ownerReferences` for webhooks)
mission.description: fix using KNative with ArgoCD (don't set `ownerReferences` for webhooks). Community-reported issue.

steps.title: Identify knative troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your knative deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n knative -l app.kubernetes.io/name=knative
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=knative -n knative --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review knative configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant knative configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n knative -l app.kubernetes.io/name=knative
kubectl get configmap -n knative -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=knative
```
In 2021 KNative made it so that the `ValidatingWebhookConfiguration` and `MutatingWebhookConfiguration` resources were "owned" by the Namespace which KNative is installed into (`kanative-serving` by default):
-

steps.title: Apply the fix for fix using KNative with ArgoCD (don't set `ownerReferences`…
steps.description:
This happened to us today.

Here's the fix:

Add `WEBHOOK_DISABLE_NAMESPACE_OWNERSHIP` env var to `webhook` container in `knative-serving` namespace.

Then commands ran:
```
kubectl delete MutatingWebhookConfiguration   "webhook.serving.knative.dev"
kubectl delete ValidatingWebhookConfiguration
```yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: xxxxxx
  namespace: xxxxxx
  ownerReferences:
    - apiVersion: apps/v1
      blockOwnerDeletion: true
      controller: true
      kind: ReplicaSet
      name: xxxxxx-759d8cb89
      uid: xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx
```

steps.title: Confirm fix using KNative with ArgoCD (don't set… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=knative -n knative --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n knative --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
This happened to us today.

Here's the fix:

Add `WEBHOOK_DISABLE_NAMESPACE_OWNERSHIP` env var to `webhook` container in `knative-serving` namespace.
codeSnippets:
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: xxxxxx
  namespace: xxxxxx
  ownerReferences:
    - apiVersion: apps/v1
      blockOwnerDeletion: true
      controller: true
      kind: ReplicaSet
      name: xxxxxx-759d8cb89
      uid: xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx

patches:
  - patch: |-
      apiVersion: apps/v1
      kind: Deployment
      metadata:
        name: REPLACED_DURING_PATCH
      spec:
        template:
          spec:
            containers:
              - name: webhook
                env:
                  - name: WEBHOOK_DISABLE_NAMESPACE_OWNERSHIP
                    value: "true"
    target:
      group: apps
      kind: Deployment
      name: ".*webhook.*"

kubectl delete MutatingWebhookConfiguration   "webhook.serving.knative.dev"
kubectl delete ValidatingWebhookConfiguration "validation.webhook.serving.knative.dev"
kubectl delete ValidatingWebhookConfiguration "config.webhook.serving.knative.dev"

kubectl delete certificate.networking.internal.knative.dev -n knative-serving         routing-serving-certs
kubectl delete certificate.networking.internal.knative.dev -n knative-serving-ingress routing-serving-certs

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with knative installed or the issue environment reproducible.
